topic: Agentic AI Research Automation: from paper reading to experiment execution

method_matrix.title: POLARIS: Typed Planning and Governed Execution for Agentic AI in Back-Office Automation
method_matrix.method: POLARIS is a modular orchestration framework for back-office automation that treats the process as planning over typed agents, using a Chain-of-Action planner for type-checked DAGs, a rubric-based selector for policy-aware selection, and guarded execution with validators and compiled policy checks.

method_matrix.title: Towards trustworthy agentic AI: a comprehensive survey of safety, robustness, privacy, and system security
method_matrix.method: The paper presents a comprehensive survey methodology that systematically examines trustworthy agentic AI through two core dimensions (Safety and Robustness, Privacy and System Security), identifying risks across agent workflows, and consolidating evaluation into a unified metrics-and-benchmarks framework.
limitations: self-evolving agents
runtime monitoring and verification
privacy-preserving personalization
trust-utility trade-off

method_matrix.title: Security, privacy, and agentic AI in a regulatory view: From definitions and distinctions to provisions and reflections
method_matrix.method: The paper is a literature review and conceptual analysis, not a technical study with a specific method.
limitations: The paper acknowledges conceptual ambiguity around key regulatory terms.

method_matrix.title: Securing the Agent: Vendor-Neutral, Multitenant Enterprise Retrieval and Tool Use
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Securing the Agent: Vendor-Neutral, Multitenant Enterprise Retrieval and Tool Use

method_matrix.title: Trustworthy AI in the Agentic Lakehouse: from Concurrency to Governance
method_matrix.method: Proposes using MVCC principles to manage concurrency in lakehouses for AI agents, with data and compute isolation through a unified API to simplify governance challenges.
baselines_compared: monolithic SQL-only transactional databases like Postgres
method_matrix.best_result: Not applicable - this is a position paper without empirical results
limitations: A direct mapping of existing techniques from traditional databases will not work in a distributed, heterogeneous system such as a lakehouse

method_matrix.title: Socio-technical aspects of Agentic AI
method_matrix.method: The paper presents a socio-technical analysis of agentic AI using the MAD-BAD-SAD construct as an analytical lens, examining how architectural choices in perception, cognition, planning, execution, and memory introduce dependencies related to data governance, accountability, transparency, safety, and sustainability.
limitations: Societal, ethical, economic, environmental, and governance implications of agentic AI remain weakly integrated into technical treatments

method_matrix.title: Caging the Agents: A Zero Trust Security Architecture for Autonomous AI in Healthcare
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Caging the Agents: A Zero Trust Security Architecture for Autonomous AI in Healthcare

method_matrix.title: PaperOrchestra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Automated AI Research Paper Writing
method_matrix.method: Research paper: PaperOrchestra: A Multi-Agent Framework for Automated AI Research Paper Writing

method_matrix.title: Real-World Gaps in AI Governance Research
method_matrix.method: Analyzed 1,178 safety and reliability papers from 9,439 generative AI papers written by 5 AI companies and 6 universities using OpenAI's o3-mini for classification and regex keyword searches for high-risk domains
baselines_compared: Corporate AI vs Academic AI research impact
method_matrix.best_result: AI governance research is highly concentrated within a handful of uniquely resourced and integrated AI tech companies

key_results.citation_concentration: Google DeepMind has more citations than top 4 academic institutions combined

method_matrix.title: Foundations of GenIR
method_matrix.method: The paper describes a conceptual framework for integrating generative AI into information access systems, focusing on two paradigms: direct information generation and information synthesis.
baselines_compared: Traditional information retrieval systems such as search engines and recommendation platforms
limitations: Traditional systems fall short for less common (long-tail) information needs and creative creation scenarios.

method_matrix.title: Claw AI Lab: An Autonomous Multi-Agent Research Team
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Claw AI Lab: An Autonomous Multi-Agent Research Team

method_matrix.title: Exploring utilization of generative AI for research and education in data-driven materials science
method_matrix.method: The authors organized a hackathon to explore the use of generative AI for research and education in materials science, focusing on participants' ability to critically evaluate and use AI-generated content for software trials and development.

method_matrix.title: Looking Forward: Challenges and Opportunities in Agentic AI Reliability
method_matrix.method: This paper is a chapter discussing research challenges and opportunities in agentic AI reliability, focusing on topics like cascading failures, dynamic environments, and testing methodologies.
limitations: The paper acknowledges that reliability mechanisms can be resource-intensive.

method_matrix.title: Paper2Agent: Reimagining Research Papers As Interactive and Reliable AI Agents
method_matrix.method: Paper2Agent transforms static research papers into interactive AI agents by extracting the associated codebase, setting up a reproducible environment, wrapping core analytical features as MCP (Model Context Protocol) tools, and deploying a remote server that an AI agent can connect to for natural language interaction with the paper's methods.

method_matrix.title: Right-to-Act: A Pre-Execution Non-Compensatory Decision Protocol for AI Systems
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Right-to-Act: A Pre-Execution Non-Compensatory Decision Protocol for AI Systems

method_matrix.title: Sola-Visibility-ISPM: Benchmarking Agentic AI for Identity Security Posture Management Visibility
method_matrix.method: The paper introduces a benchmark called Sola-Visibility-ISPM to evaluate agentic AI systems on Identity Security Posture Management tasks, defining nine operational dimensions for assessment.

method_matrix.title: Beyond principlism: Practical strategies for ethical AI use in research practices
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Beyond principlism: Practical strategies for ethical AI use in research practices

method_matrix.title: SBOMs into Agentic AIBOMs: Schema Extensions, Agentic Orchestration, and Reproducibility Evaluation
method_matrix.method: Research paper: SBOMs into Agentic AIBOMs: Schema Extensions, Agentic Orchestration, and Reproducibility Evaluation

method_matrix.title: Persistent AI Agents in Academic Research: A Single-Investigator Implementation Case Study
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Persistent AI Agents in Academic Research: A Single-Investigator Implementation Case Study

method_matrix.title: Safe, Untrusted, "Proof-Carrying" AI Agents: toward the agentic lakehouse
method_matrix.method: The paper introduces programmable lakehouse abstractions for modeling the data lifecycle, operationalizes a prototype for pipeline repair using a ReAct loop, and addresses safety objections for high-stakes automation.
limitations: The prototype is presented as a feasibility demonstration rather than a full-fledged experimental benchmark

method_matrix.title: Architectures for Building Agentic AI
method_matrix.method: Proposes agentic architectures that reconcile traditional autonomous agent concepts with modern generative models, focusing on reliability through componentisation, interfaces, and supervision.
baselines_compared: Traditional autonomous agents (reactive, deliberative, BDI architectures)

method_matrix.title: AEMA: Verifiable Evaluation Framework for Trustworthy and Controlled Agentic LLM Systems
method_matrix.method: Research paper: AEMA: Verifiable Evaluation Framework for Trustworthy and Controlled Agentic LLM Systems

method_matrix.title: A cybersecurity AI agent selection and decision support framework
method_matrix.method: The study employs a granular decomposition of NIST CSF 2.0 functions into specific tasks, linking essential AI agent properties (autonomy, adaptive learning, real-time responsiveness) to subcategory security requirements, and outlines graduated levels of autonomy (assisted, augmented, fully autonomous).

method_matrix.title: First head-to-head comparison of agentic AI applied to the analysis of simulated data of the Einstein Telescope
method_matrix.method: A head-to-head comparison of two agentic AI systems (Claude Code and Codex) in performing a complete scientific analysis pipeline on simulated gravitational wave data, from signal recovery to manuscript generation.
datasets_used: Simulated binary black hole signal injections for the Einstein Telescope
method_matrix.best_result: Claude Code completed the pipeline significantly faster (~3.4 min vs ~16 min) while achieving scientific convergence with Codex.

key_results.scientific_convergence: Both runs converged on the same scientific results

limitations: Agents exhibited substantially different behaviors and computational costs
Claude Code made silent deviations from the specification
Codex required explicit self-correcting restarts
A subtle difference in SNR interpretation led to genuine scientific divergence in one run

method_matrix.title: AI for Auto-Research: Roadmap & User Guide
method_matrix.method: End-to-end analysis of AI across the complete research lifecycle, organized into four epistemological phases: Creation, Writing, Validation, and Dissemination, studying developments through April 2026.
limitations: AI systems still fabricate results under scientific pressure
AI systems miss hidden errors
AI systems fail to judge novelty reliably
Generated ideas often degrade after implementation
Research code lags behind pattern-matching benchmarks
End-to-end autonomous systems haven't consistently reached major-venue acceptance standards
Greater automation can obscure rather than eliminate failure modes

method_matrix.title: Towards Execution-Grounded Automated AI Research
method_matrix.method: The authors built a high-throughput automated executor to implement and evaluate LLM-generated research ideas in parallel, using the execution results as feedback to guide an evolutionary search and reinforcement learning process for the ideator model.
method_matrix.best_result: Execution-guided evolutionary search found a post-training recipe outperforming the GRPO baseline by 21.4 percentage points on math reasoning.
limitations: RL causes the ideator model to converge on a few easy-to-implement ideas, resulting in a collapse in thinking length and idea diversity.
Only Claude-4.5-Opus shows a clear scaling curve, while both Claude-4.5-Sonnet and GPT-5 tend to saturate early.
method_matrix.compute_required: GPU-intensive (implied by LLM pre-training and post-training on 1.5B models)

method_matrix.title: The Denario project: Deep knowledge AI agents for scientific discovery
method_matrix.method: The paper applies methods from quantum physics and machine learning to astrophysical data.
limitations: The provided text does not contain a limitations section.
The provided text does not contain a results section.

method_matrix.title: AutoResearch AI: Towards AI-Powered Research Automation for Scientific Discovery
method_matrix.method: This paper presents a survey and framework for AutoResearch, analyzing the spectrum of AI-powered scientific workflow automation from human-steered 'Vibe Research' to fully autonomous AI-led systems.
limitations: AutoResearch autonomy is domain-conditioned, being more credible in structured, executable, and rapidly verifiable settings but limited in embodied, delayed, heterogeneous, ethical, or institutionally accountable contexts.

method_matrix.title: AI-Researcher: Autonomous Scientific Innovation
method_matrix.method: Research paper: AI-Researcher: Autonomous Scientific Innovation

method_matrix.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
method_matrix.method: Research paper: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery

method_matrix.title: Robin: A multi-agent system for automating scientific discovery
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Robin: A multi-agent system for automating scientific discovery

method_matrix.title: The Future of Scientific Publishing: Automated Article Generation
method_matrix.method: The paper describes a software tool that generates academic articles by analyzing Python code and datasets, without using advanced language models.
limitations: The paper does not explicitly state any limitations.
method_matrix.code_url: Not specified in the provided text
method_matrix.compute_required: Not specified in the provided text

method_matrix.title: Experiment-as-Code Labs: A Declarative Stack for AI-Driven Scientific Discovery
method_matrix.method: A declarative stack that encodes experiments as configurations, which are compiled to device-level APIs for AI-driven scientific discovery.
limitations: The paper acknowledges that Eureka moments could occur at any time while operating lab instruments, which may not be fully captured by the proposed automated system.

method_matrix.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
method_matrix.method: The AI Scientist-v2 uses a progressive agentic tree-search methodology managed by an experiment manager agent, eliminates human-authored code templates, and integrates a Vision-Language Model feedback loop for figure refinement.
baselines_compared: The AI Scientist-v1 (Lu et al., 2024 arXiv:2408.06292)
method_matrix.best_result: One manuscript exceeded the average human acceptance threshold at ICLR workshop peer review

method_matrix.title: Honest Lying: Understanding Memory Confabulation in Reflexive Agents
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Honest Lying: Understanding Memory Confabulation in Reflexive Agents

method_matrix.title: Explainable AI for Blind and Low-Vision Users: Navigating Trust, Modality, and Interpretability in the Agentic Era
method_matrix.method: Conceptual analysis of XAI challenges for BLV users, identifying the modality gap and proposing accessible XAI approaches

method_matrix.title: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Reward Modeling
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Mitigating Perceptual Judgment Bias in Multimodal LLM-as-a-Judge via Perceptual Perturbation and Rew

method_matrix.title: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Continual Instruction Tuning
method_matrix.method: Research paper: ProtoAda: Prototype-Guided Adaptive Adapter Expansion and Geometric Consolidation for Multimodal Con

method_matrix.title: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs
method_matrix.method: Research paper: AdaCodec: A Predictive Visual Code for Video MLLMs

method_matrix.title: ClinEnv: An Interactive Multi-Stage Long Horizon EHR Environment for Agents
method_matrix.method: ClinEnv is an interactive benchmark that evaluates LLMs as attending physicians through Longitudinal Inpatient Simulation, where models must query four specialized agents at each decision stage before committing to medications, procedures, and diagnoses.
method_matrix.best_result: 0.31 decision F1 for the strongest model
limitations: Models struggle with management decisions
Models continue to issue redundant queries as cases progress
Information-acquisition gap is invisible to outcome-only evaluation

method_matrix.title: IntraShuffler: A Privacy Preserving Framework for Heterogeneous DP Federated Learning
method_matrix.method: Research paper: IntraShuffler: A Privacy Preserving Framework for Heterogeneous DP Federated Learning

method_matrix.title: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assured Interactive Robotics
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Permissive Safety Through Trusted Inference: Verifiable Belief-Space Neural Safety Filters for Assur

method_matrix.title: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression
method_matrix.method: Research paper: From Layers to Submodules: Rethinking Granularity in Replacement-Based LLM Compression

method_matrix.title: Modeling Depth Ambiguity: A Mixture-Density Representation for Flying-Point-Free Depth Estimation
method_matrix.method: MDA (Mixture-Density Representation) predicts multiple depth hypotheses and their probabilities per pixel to address depth ambiguity at boundaries, extending naturally to transparent objects and sky regions.

method_matrix.title: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models
method_matrix.method: Research paper: SimSD: Simple Speculative Decoding in Diffusion Language Models

method_matrix.title: Tracking the Behavioral Trajectories of Adapting Agents
method_matrix.method: They define traits as directions in text embedding space, train a linear model on labeled skill diffs, and score skill edits by projecting embedding diffs onto trait vectors.
datasets_used: 68 labeled skill diff pairs for the trait of propensity to seek sensitive data
method_matrix.best_result: 91.2% sign classification accuracy on 68 labeled skill diff pairs

method_matrix.title: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment
method_matrix.method: Research paper: SafeSteer: Localized On-Policy Distillation for Efficient Safety Alignment

method_matrix.title: Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Representations and Portfolio Allocation
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Auditing Asset-Specific Preferences in Financial Large Language Models: Evidence from Bitcoin Repres

method_matrix.title: Why Not Hyperparameter-Friendly Optimisation? A Monotonic Adaptive Norm Rescaling Approach For Long-Tailed Recognition
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Why Not Hyperparameter-Friendly Optimisation? A Monotonic Adaptive Norm Rescaling Approach For Long-

method_matrix.title: Moment-Video: Diagnosing Temporal Fidelity of Video MLLMs on Momentary Visual Events
method_matrix.method: Introduces Moment-Video, a benchmark with 1,000 human-verified video-QA pairs across 7 domains and 25 subcategories, covering four task types: Temporal Occurrence, Temporal Counting, Action Description, and Temporal Reasoning.
limitations: Denser frame sampling improves some models but does not eliminate the bottleneck
Longer videos introduce stronger temporal-localization challenges
Current video MLLMs lack temporally faithful representations for capturing, preserving, and using brief but decisive visual evidence

method_matrix.title: Drifting Preference Optimization for One-Step Generative Models
method_matrix.method: DrPO samples candidates from the current generator, ranks them with a target reward, and uses high- and low-scoring samples to synthesize a feature-space update direction consisting of a non-parametric dipole preference field plus reference drift.
method_matrix.best_result: Reduces HPSv3 training computation by 3.51× under matched effective-batch setting

method_matrix.title: A Biconvex Formulation for Stable Transport of Mixture Models with a Unique Solution
method_matrix.method: Optimal Mixture Transport (OMT) reformulates the transport problem as a strictly biconvex optimization over mixture models, decoupling computational complexity from sample size.

method_matrix.title: Towards Automated Discovery: A Review of Generative Models, Multimodal Learning and Closed-Loop Workflows in Inverse Materials Design
method_matrix.method: Survey and comparative analysis of generative models for inverse materials design, examining variational autoencoders, normalizing flows, autoregressive formulations, and diffusion models with multimodal learning approaches.
limitations: surrogate exploitation
diversity collapse
distribution shift
stability-synthesizability gap

method_matrix.title: Bridging the Last Mile of Time Series Forecasting with LLM Agents
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Bridging the Last Mile of Time Series Forecasting with LLM Agents

method_matrix.title: Monitoring Agentic Systems Before They're Reliable
method_matrix.method: Three-tiered monitoring approach (within-run, cross-run, structural) using variance-based characterization with FMEA-inspired severity classification to prioritize findings
datasets_used: synthetic testbed with 220 runs across 120 document bundles
method_matrix.best_result: Structural monitor identifies integration gap with perfect consistency (CV = 0.00)
limitations: specific calibrations are domain-specific

method_matrix.title: Expressivity of congruence-based architectures for DNNs on positive-definite matrices
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Expressivity of congruence-based architectures for DNNs on positive-definite matrices

method_matrix.title: RASER: Recoverability-Aware Selective Escalation Router for Multi-Hop Question Answering
method_matrix.method: Research paper: RASER: Recoverability-Aware Selective Escalation Router for Multi-Hop Question Answering

method_matrix.title: Iteris: Agentic Research Loops for Computational Mathematics
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Iteris: Agentic Research Loops for Computational Mathematics

method_matrix.title: Ghost Tool Calls: Issue-Time Privacy for Speculative Agent Tools
method_matrix.method: Implementation of Speculative Tool Privacy Contracts as a runtime abstraction to handle observation before commitment as a first-class effect, distinct from state mutation.
limitations: no commit-time cleanup
read-only restriction
access-control allow-list unsends what an observer already holds

method_matrix.title: Physics-Informed Residuals for Adaptive Mesh Refinement in Finite-Difference PDE Solvers
method_matrix.method: Uses Physics-Informed Neural Network residuals as cellwise indicators for adaptive mesh refinement in finite-difference PDE solvers
method_matrix.best_result: PINN-threshold refinement achieves relative L² error 0.021067 with 60 degrees of freedom, compared to 0.022617 for uniform refinement with 192 degrees of freedom
limitations: Gradient indicator remains slightly more accurate
PINN residuals don't consistently outperform gradient or uniform baselines in 2D/3D tests

method_matrix.title: MCP-Persona: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Real-World Personal Applications via Environment Simulation
method_matrix.method: Research paper: MCP-Persona: Benchmarking LLM Agents on Real-World Personal Applications via Environment Simulation

method_matrix.title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
method_matrix.method: Agent Laboratory is a framework that uses LLM agents to automate the research process in three stages: literature review, experimentation, and report writing, based on a human-provided research idea.
method_matrix.best_result: Agent Laboratory driven by o1-preview generates the best research outcomes and achieves state-of-the-art performance in machine learning code.

key_results.research_expense_reduction: 84% decrease compared to previous autonomous research methods

limitations: Human involvement, providing feedback at each stage, significantly improves the overall quality of research.

method_matrix.method: Research paper: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning

method_matrix.method: Research paper: Self-Refine: Iterative Refinement with Self-Feedback

method_matrix.title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
method_matrix.method: Research paper: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models

method_matrix.title: AgentBench: Evaluating LLMs as Agents
method_matrix.method: AgentBench is a multi-dimensional benchmark designed to evaluate LLM-as-Agent across a spectrum of different environments, encompassing eight distinct environments categorized into three types of groundings: Code, Game, and Web.
limitations: Lack of a systematic and standard benchmark to evaluate LLM-as-Agent
Previous text-based game environments suffer from closed, discrete action spaces
Previous embodied agent simulators don't accurately reflect practical use cases of LLMs
Most benchmarks focus on single environments and fail to provide comprehensive overview

method_matrix.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
method_matrix.method: Research paper: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
